domain: Greek hoplite phalanx (interlocking aspis-shield wall — each soldier's shield overlaps and protects the LEFT half of his right-side neighbor's torso; the wall is a continuous, overlap-coupled barrier; othismos = collective forward push)
specific_mechanism: Each hoplite's aspis covers his own left side AND his right-neighbor's right side. The wall is non-redundant: it works only because every shield contributes both self-protection and neighbor-protection. Failure of any soldier (drop shield, fall) breaks the wall locally. Othismos pushing uses collective mass to break enemy line; individual hoplite cannot push effectively alone.
llm_application: Phalanx-overlap MAS defense ring: configure N LLM agents in a ring; agent_i runs a SAFETY CHECKER head over its own output AND over agent_{i-1}'s output (shield overlap on left neighbor). For an attacker-controlled prompt to reach the system, it must compromise agent_i's primary processing AND agent_{i+1}'s shield-overlap check (the agent to the right). Failure model: single-agent compromise still triggers neighbor-shield refusal. Distinct from majority voting (no contiguous-range requirement) and from independent self-check (no neighbor coverage). Hard architectural constraint enforced by message-routing topology, not by training.
novelty_claim: BlindGuard (2508.08127), PeerGuard (2505.11642), G-Safeguard (2502.11127) use detection / mutual-reasoning / topology-anomaly. None proposes the specific phalanx OVERLAP topology with hard ring-constraint requiring contiguous-range neighbor concurrence. BFT-style threshold voting (2/3 quorum) is not contiguous-range.
